publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-11-03 00:08:52 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes correspondence, research projects, civil rights material, reports, fund raising material, programs, minutes of meetings, financial records, memoranda, press releases, newsletters, publications, correspondence and other material related to various Republican organizations, mailings to potential contributors and subscribers, membership records, research materials and papers, clippings, and other records of the Ripon Society. Also records of the Ripon Society collected by Howard F. Gillette, Jr.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, research projects, civil rights material, reports, fund raising material, programs, minutes of meetings, financial records, memoranda, press releases, newsletters, publications, correspondence and other material related to various Republican organizations, mailings to potential contributors and subscribers, membership records, research materials and papers, clippings, and other records of the Ripon Society. Also records of the Ripon Society collected by Howard F. Gillette, Jr.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Ripon Society records, #2824. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to all additions to the collection restricted for 10 years from the date of arrival at Cornell with the exception of publications and news releases. Access to Boxes 31-53 restricted until November 2019.

unittitle.extref: Link to a copy of the printed finding aid

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bruce K. Chapman to J. Eugene Marans, regarding Ripon's work in civil rights

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include membership in Ripon Society, formation of Ripon Society Charters in New York City and other cities; requests for Ripon publications; comments on the "rebirth" of Republican Party and the candidacy of Barry Goldwater

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include requests for publications and membership information; Roger H. Woodworth (Edward Brooke staff) to John S. Saloma, regarding Mass. Politics; Vern Cougill, Helena Montana to Ripon Society re: Montana political affairs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include membership applications; requests for publications; speakers for meetings at Ripon Headquarters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include membership, publications; desire for unity within the Republican Party, and information on the Lincoln Research Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include membership and publications; Steve McCarthy (Tom McCall staff) to Ted S. Curtis, re: McCall's campaign for Governor of Oregon; John R. Price to Roy H. Millenson (U.S. Senate Committee on Labor and Public Welfare) re: higher education and Medicare bills; Leonard J. Nadasey (General Mills, Minneapolis) to Ted S. Curtis, re: the "Oxford Conference" and Young Republicans; unity in the Republican Party

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include future direction of the Republican Party; inquiries re: membership and publications; Sen. Hugh Scott, Pennsylvania, and his book on Republican Party; John R. Price to Gov. Nelson A. Rockefeller, re: razing of historic buildings and possibility of Jackie Robinson as a candidate for public office

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include federal programs for distressed areas; inquiries about membership and Ripon publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Membership and publications; see Mary McInnis letter for information on John V. Lindsay and Ombudsman plan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Membership and publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Membership and publications; Speaker at Ripon Society meeting; Larry Landis, Indianapolis re: mayoralty candidacy of Richard Lugar; Negative income tax plan; open letter to all $100. contributors outlining research work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposed research topics; Latin American project, China paper; Foreign aid project; Ripon - Grinnell College research; Negative income tax project; Guaranteed annual wage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research project of Ripon Society. Coordinated by J. Eugene Marans; comments by David A. Martin, Geneseo, N.Y.

did.unittitle: Baker, Sen. Howard H., Jr. Research Project

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes papers on: Youth, balance of payments, Latin America, Conservation, Crime, NATO, intellectuals, and GOP. Correspondence includes H.W. Baker, Jr. congratulating R.S. on papers; Melvin H. Bernstein (Beverly Hills, Calif., R.S. of Southern California); Thomas A. Brown (Rand Corp.); Roger P. Craig (Wash. D.C.), commenting on crime paper; Fred M. Lowenfels (Scarsdale), commenting on conservation paper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: Position papers, including privacy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Robert D. Behn to R.K. Price - 2 pp. re: possible research papers for R. Nixon; Pat. J. Buchanan (Nixon staff) to Tim E. Petri, RE: "cute phrase" project; R.D. Behn Memo to staff on "cute phrase".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding position papers, including low income housing, capital punishment. Includes Percy to R.D. Behn on National Home Ownership Foundation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: Position papers, including urban problems, senior citizens, inflation, small business

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily Robert D. Behn re: research projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and drafts re: J. Eugene Morans; Joseph W. Harrison (staff of Repres. Remecke, Calif.) Elmer L. Andersen (St. Paul); Nelson L. Crowther, Jr. (Wellesley, Mass.); Albert E. Abrahams (Republicans for Congress)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and comments on war safety report for Congress. F. Bradford Morse (Mass.). Morton H. Halperin (Harvard) to N.F. Emmons, unfavorable comments re: report; Alton Frye (Harvard) to Emmons unfavorable reaction to the report.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence membership, redevelopment of Virginia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memorandum re: brutality by J.E. Marans and John Wardell 13 pp. Eugene Marans to Bob Particelli 3 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: Mental Health. Paper by T. Petri with some comments re: draft. (not included); Also Petri to Nat Emmons discusses other research including ombudsman, civil rights, taxation, aged, and youth

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes find raising brochures, Eugene J. McCarthy 1962, 1965: N.A.A.C.P.; T.E. Petri to J. Saloma on ideas for fund raising; Repub. for Progress; Republican Citizens Committee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes contributors names, addresses and some financial amounts for: William S. Beinecke, NYC; Mrs Herbert Brownell, NYC; John H. Hauberg, Seattle; Andrew Heiskell, NYC; Amory Houghton, Corning; Jacob K. Javits; Henry Cabot Lodge; Mrs Oswald B. Lord, NYC; Thomas B. McCabe, Philadelphia; J. Willard Marriott, Washington; Richard M. Nixon; William S. Paley. Correspondence with Walter N. Thayer, N.Y.C. re: finances of Ripon Society; Memo on Keating Test Mailing; Nov. 10, 1964. Gordon Grossman to W.N. Thayer and A. L. Cole; also George W. Romney; Hugh Scott; Nelson A. Rockefeller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Amory Houghton, Jr. Corning; John B. Martin, Grand Rapids, Michigan; J.S. Saloma to John H. Whitney; J.S. Saloma to W.N. Thayer; J.S. Saloma - Ripon Finances, 2 pp. memo to Exec. Bd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes agendas, summary of meetings, minutes of meetings. Confidential agenda and proposal for American Bow Group 3 pp.; Summary Minutes, Name Ripon Society chosen; meeting about Republican National Committee; Richard Nixon, work of Ripon Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "The Idea for the Ripon Society" 3 pp. objectives, present status, plans; "Ripon's History" - Harvard Law School Bulletin p. 6-7. The Ripon Society. A History and prospectus, Cambridge, Mass. Copy, "Gadfly of G.O.P.: Ripon Society Success Story" Milw. Journal. Biographical sketch of T.E. Petri and other officers of Ripon Society; Articles of Organization, Mass.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "A Program and a Budget for the Ripon Society," 8 pp. includes a 4 year projection. Treas. Report 8 pp.;Financial Statement and Prospectus 3 pp.; Finance Matters by Mike Stansbury 4 pp.; Annual Report 9 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Projected Lines for Development" 6 pp.; J. Saloma to "Riponer" re: San Francisco 1964 Convention. The Ripon Society and the 1964 elections; pre-election activities and interim budget 4 pp.; Emil Frankel to Riponer, review of current activities 3 pp.; Expansion of National membership and organizational liaison 5 pp.; "Ripon Society, Philosophy, Goals, and Strategies" 5 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memo by C.T. Bayley to J.E. Marans outlining activities of Ripon Society 8 pp.; From J. Saloma on fund raising, book by Sen. Hugh Scott and various research projects 4pp.; J. Saloma, T. Petri, and Ted Curtis re: "From Disaster to Distinction," Forum and research activities 4 pp.; Ripon Society in California 3 pp.; Peter J. Wallison re: financial statement and prospectus 3pp.; J. Saloma re: Ripon Forum and its development 5pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Agenda - meetings, 1st meeting 3 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Summary Minutes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, Memos, proposed amendments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Members

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Resolutions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes of Meetings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence: Congressman Gerald R. Ford; Otto A. Silha (Minneapolis); Cong. Robert F. Ellsworth (Kansas); Mark O. Hatfield; Al E. Abrahams; Elmer L. Andersen (St. Paul; Robert H. Finch (Calif.); David Goldberg (Boston); Vera Glaser (Wash. D.C.); Hastings Keith (Mass); Steven H. Hess (Wash. D.C.); Herbert Noble (Minneapolis); Dwight D. Eisenhower; John H. Chafee (R. Island); Thomas E. Petri; John R. Price (NYC); Walter N. Thayer; Lee W. Huebner

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mainly letters re: his Peace Corp work in Mogadiscio, Somalia, East Africa; provide detailed information on his legal activities, including re-drafting Somali Civil Service Law and translating the Somali Civil Code from Italian to English; George Romney on Election '64 Report; T.E. Petri on Wisconsin politics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, name of possible members

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: formation of a Ripon Chapter in Northwest; memos re: Ripon meetings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: formation of chapter; includes William C. Ives to J.S. Saloma of Feb 6 meeting in Chicago

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: formation of chapter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes correspondence Robert H. Finch, Robert D. Fagoly, Jr.; re: Ripon activities in California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: Ripon, possible chapter, with Robert Womark

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: Ripon, possible chapter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Ripon Society" by the A.C.U. 31 pp.

did.unittitle: Association of Republicans for Educated Action (AREA) Philadelphia
Bull Moose Club, Mass. Inst. Technology

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clipping, Christian S. Monitor re: Bull Moose; Bull Moose Platform 7pp.; Election Analyses 5pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: work of the Foundation; primarily financial studies of political parties

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Formed in Jan 1965, progressive organization; correspondence centers on a manifesto Ripon asked to write for the committee. Correspondence include Warren J. Sinsheimer, Rita E. Hauser; also includes drafts of manifesto and speech of W.J. Sinsheimer, before Frontier Club of Republican Women, Buffalo, New York.

did.unittitle: Committee for Forward Looking Republicans, Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Press Release asking for resignation of Dean Burch and John Grenier from Republican National Committee. Also, a membership list of the committee. 16 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Circular letter" and press release.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence relates to work of Compass - moderate and liberal - with Ripon; Correspondents include David V. O'Brien, and Michael o. Sawyer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: meetings, comments on meetings; Russell G. Simpson, (Boston) to J.S. Saloma 18 pp. on CRO and future of Republican party

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes of Meetings and news releases.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Press releases, clippings, Congressional Record; re: "Morality in Selma - Selma Crisis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed Program of E.C.O. meeting; List of Conferees for meeting; "Tools of the Trade" speech by Richard A. Armstrong, Ex. Director E.C.O., before American Iron & Steel Institute.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One letter - Joseph McMahon to Joel Fisher re: the establishment of Haven in Michigan; law students at University of Michigan interested in Republican Party

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newsletter re: governmental affairs (Introd. Issue); Gordon Weller, Grand Junction, Colorado, founder, to J.S. Saloma re: "Iconoclast"

did.unittitle: Thomas Jefferson Research Center, Pasadena, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publications of the independent research organization, specializing in "universal human solutions" Includes booklet re: work of the Center and six one-page newsletters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Meeting notice 3 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposal to establish J.M.S. by John McClaughry; Constitution and Bylaws.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: Advance, volunteer professional & business people, particularly interested in minority groups and work in Ripon 4 items

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Announcements and meetings - group composed of Republican staff and met weekly on Capitol Hill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Ben A. Webster, Jr. (Des Moines) and newsletter for the organization.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ray C. Bliss correspondence (routine) and clippings re: work of chairman Bliss

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous correspondence includes Mrs Russell T. Lund, Minneapolis, to Ripon Society giving encouragement to Ripon Society; Memo for files, J.S. Saloma re: lunch with Dr. Arthur Peterson, R.N.C. 3 pp.; Summary Minutes, Ripon Meeting, Ray Humphreys, R.N.C. - Education, re: Ripon, Republican Party and South, Negro and in Virginia 3 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The 1964 Elections" 75 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various printed, campaign materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed material, membership lists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Election Report & Returns; Candidates for Governor, House and Senate; Election Fact Sheet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Precinct Action course 95 pp. and Leaders Manual, P.A. Course 100 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Founded Feb 1965 (formerly committee to support moderate republicans). Correspondence includes John McClaughry to J.S. Saloma re: moderate Republican organization; Al E. Abrahams to Theodore S. Curtis, Jr., re: Republican women; Charles P. Taft to J.S. Saloma re: financial problems of R.F.P.; Memo, R.F.P. re: June 9th meeting; Anthony C. Morella, American University to Charles P. Taft, 3 pp. on Republican activities in Maryland.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed materials, news releases, clippings re: R.F.P.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence include Daniel J. Evans; Robert E. Smylie. Memo for files by J.S. Saloma re: Republican Coordination Committee meeting in S.C. - relates how Gov. Thomas E. Dewey redrafted statement on civil rights and Vietnam.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes news releases, and drafts of report to R.G.A. re: "Third Force" in Republican Party

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Republicans (progressive) who are law and graduate students, plus professors at Columbia University. Includes correspondence position papers on the "tax cut and a policy of full employment" and "Synergistic Federalism"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Republican National Committee staff member and 1964 attempt to divert Negro votes from Democratic party

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes press list, Forum, Jan 1965, April 1965 and correspondence with Melvin H. Bernstein

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes printed matter, correspondence re: F.S.A.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: in Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous correspondence clippings re: Y.R. in Wisconsin, New York, Mass.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily correspondence, reports, and related materials concerning New Jersey Young Republicans and "Rat Fink" extremism charges

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, news releases re: Young Republicans and concentrating on "Rat Fink" episode within Young Republicans from New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous newsletters, clippings, mostly relating to New Jersey "Rat Fink", racist and anti-religious activities

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Directory of Members; G.O.P. Party Press (Newsletter of Pa. Young Republicans)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence re: civil rights conference in Michigan; Final report of the University Young Republicans ad hoc Committee on Civil Rights 12 pp.; Correspondence re: summer program dealing with inner-city problems including letters from George Romney and John V. Lindsay to David C. Miller, Young Republicans, Michigan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous correspondence re: Republican activities in Iowa, Connecticut, Mississippi, New York, Minnesota, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from John B. Talmadge re: O.C.C.R. work and candidates in Oregon; Camden M. Hall to C.T. Bayley and Ripon Society, 1965, re: O.C.C.R. and Dorchester Conference (April 9-11, 1965) 4 pp.; Membership list for O.C.C.R, Roster 2 pp., and newsletters (4)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: work of N.C.R.W. but mostly printed matter (Bulletins) from Republican Workshop of Minnesota

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Routine correspondence re: Ripon and Fred Van Natta, Ex. Director R.S.L.A.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "The Republican Party in Washington's Future" by A.L. Kramer, George Kinnear, and Don Moos 9 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relates to committee members from various states, including North Dakota, Ohio, Michigan, Oklahoma, Connecticut, Colorado, Wyoming, Missouri, Rhode Island, Illinois, and Louisiana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of Lindsay contributors (over $250.) in 1965 12 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, miscellaneous printed matter re: possibility of Gavin for President

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Massachusetts Republican Citizens movement which is concerned with voter survey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: List of Delegates and Alternates; also, annotated list re: William V. Scranton and used in Ripon Society financial drive

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newsletters (4) dated 1965

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (not marked on folders) Ripon designation retained

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (not marked on folders) Ripon designation retained

scopecontent.p: (not marked on folders) Ripon designation retained

scopecontent.p: (not marked on folders) Ripon designation retained

did.unittitle: Michigan, membership, re: possible Presidential choice of Republican Party

scopecontent.p: (not marked on folders) Ripon designation retained

scopecontent.p: (not marked on folders) Ripon designation retained

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mailing to potential contributors and subscribers using advance mailing lists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters of solicitation and returns

did.unittitle: Advance magazine - conclusion of above project

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Liberty Letter; National Review; Moderator (article on Ripon Society)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "A Young American Looks at the Party, His Nation and Their Future", Kansas, lacks pagination

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings and articles re: Negro, conservation groups, primarily 1966

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: National Independent Committee for President Johnson and Senator Humphrey, N.Y.T.; National Committee for Repeal of the McCarran Act, N.Y.T. Congressional Record; National Independent Committee for President Johnson and Senator Humphrey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous materials re: work of Ripon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Form letters re: Ripon fund raising

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from members of press re: work of Ripon and occasional notes on political developments in the U.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: continuation of letters from members of press

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mail routing list

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re: membership, dues and observations on political development within Republican Party, mostly from Mass. or from Ripon "staff" members

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: postcard "Little White School House at Ripon, Wisc". "Grass Root Facts" (Birth of Republican Party) by Alan E. Bovay, newspaper clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Carl B. Rubin to Leonard S. Green (Ripon) on Robert Taft, Jr. campaign for Congress from Ohio 3 pp.; Taft precinct handbook 12 pp.; printed matter re: David R. Reed - candidate for Congress, Chicago 2 pp.; First Congressional District, Chicago, Illinois Confidential Report 6 pp.; Information re: Herbert R. Cain, Philadelphia and his candidacy for 2nd Congress. District of Pennsylvania

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "A Call to Excellence in Leadership" draft copies and final statement of a declaration of conscience, Ripon, Wisc. 9 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and press releases re: Ripon statement at Ripon, Wisconsin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence mostly from J.S. Saloma to various Republicans asking for their comment on "Call to Excellence in Leadership." Includes copy of a letter Dwight D. Eisenhower to Walter N. Thayer, 3 pp. in which former President discusses Republican philosophy, and Pres. John F. Kennedy's actions after the Bay of Pigs incident.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement, memo of the Ripon Society to the Republican Governors Association meeting in Los Angeles 5 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memos re: Forum includes suggestions for format from Albert L. Cole, Readers Digest; several multi-page memos on content and layout of the Forum; bi-weekly report of New Haven chapter of Forum.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ripon Forums have been removed from 180, 181, 182, 183, 184 to serials June 6, 1975 Burton Huth

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rep. Gov. Conference, Colorado

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: mentally ill

scopecontent.p: negative income tax

scopecontent.p: overkill at Omaha, Young Republicans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rep. Gov. Conference

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes-description, order forms "A Call to Excellence in Leadership" 9 pp.; "Consular Treaty, Research Memo 4 pp.; Draft, Research memo 4 pp. "Citizenship for Cubans" 2 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tax Revenue-Sharing with state and local governments 21 pp.; Ripon S. Report and analysis of 1965 election 10 pp.; Republican Civil Rights Platform for 1965, 5 pp.; Overkill at Omaha (Young Republican National Federation) 8 pp.; The Republican Governor's Association - The Case for a Third Force 20 pp.; China 7 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research memo - "The Arsenal of Weapons - Legislation to Curb Paralysis Strikes" prepared by William J. Kilberg 39 pp.

did.unittitle: Ripon Society research memos prepared for John A. Volpe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Job Opportunities 15 pp.; Housing 19 pp.; Senior Citizens 23 pp.; Automation and Technology 15 pp.; Crime 8 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: draft copy, "Here is the Rest of Him, A report on Ronald Reagan" by Michael C. Smith 77 pp.

did.unittitle: Ripon Society - Profiles in Republicanism- - proposal by John McClaughry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and draft of proposal outlining persons who could be included in a book on Republican Party

did.unittitle: Ripon Society - Joint offer of "The Party that Lost Its Head" and "From Disaster to Distinction"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: by John C. Topping, John R. Lazarel and William H. Linder

scopecontent.p: Xerox copy -bound, lacks pagination

scopecontent.p: correspondence, mostly re: copies of the report, most of them dated 1966

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thomas P. Petri, editor 124 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: correspondence, mainly with Pocket Books, Inc. regarding publishing the book and comments on the book by Riponers and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: correspondence re: possible publication of book on 1964 election

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: correspondence re: copies, delay in printings

scopecontent.p: correspondence includes comments on "From Disaster to Distinction"

did.unittitle: Task Force on Strategy and Organization
Press Releases and other related papers
A: AFL-CIO; American Conservation Union, American Medical Association; American Security Council
G: General Motors; Goldwater, Barry; Gregory, Dick
H: Hess, Karl; Hickel, Walter; Holton, Linwood; Hughes, Harold; Human Events
L: Laird, Melvin; League of Women Voters
M: Morton, Rogers C.B.; Moynihan, Daniel P.
N: NAACP - Legal Defense Fund; New Party; Nixon, P.C.
National Council for an Indochina Deadline
S: Saxbe; Scott, Hugh; Schweiker, Dick; Steiger, Bill
W: Wednesday Club; Women's Political Caucus
Conventions - New Haven Republican Campaign Platform
Conventions - Republican National Convention - Rules Fight

From Disaster to Distinction - The Rebirth of the Republican Party
The Politics of Moderation - the Ripon Papers
